slides.title: Making Things
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 1 English, Unit 4: Making Things. In this unit, your child learns the words for shapes, colours, and clothes, and how to make things with paper shapes — like a paper quilt, a picture, or a shape animal. They talk about what people are wearing and dress up in pretend party costumes. They meet the short ‘e’ sound (hen, pen, tent) and enjoy the old story of the elves and the shoemaker. The gentle idea running through it all is that making things takes creativity and patience — and it is fun to try, try again. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name and point to shapes (square, circle, triangle, rectangle)
Name and point to colours (red, blue, green, yellow, silver, gold…)
Name some clothes and costumes (hat, shirt, dress, clown, king…)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name and point to shapes (square, circle, triangle, rectangle). you will also name and point to colours (red, blue, green, yellow, silver, gold…). you will also name some clothes and costumes (hat, shirt, dress, clown, king…). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: square, circle, triangle, rectangle, red, blue. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Amal Makes a Mat and Talk about Making Things. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Language pattern 1
bullets: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: This is a ___.
slides.narration: The first language focus is Language pattern 1. Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: This is a ___. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 - Listen and point. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
